news.content:
The phpMyFAQ Team would like to announce the availability of [phpMyFAQ 4.1.4](/download), the "Luna Jordan" releases.
This release fixes security vulnerabilities, updates all third party dependencies, and fixes all reported bugs.
[Click here to find a detailed security advisory](/security/advisory-2026-05-25).

The phpMyFAQ Team is pleased to announce [phpMyFAQ 4.1.3](/download), the "Alessandro Zanardi" releases.
This release fixes security vulnerabilities, updates all third party dependencies, and fixes all reported bugs.
[Click here to find a detailed security advisory](/security/advisory-2026-05-14).

The phpMyFAQ Team would like to announce the availability of [phpMyFAQ 4.1.2](/download), the "Mario Adorf" releases.
This release fixes security vulnerabilities, updates all third party dependencies, and fixes all reported bugs.
[Click here to find a detailed security advisory](/security/advisory-2026-04-28).

The phpMyFAQ Team is pleased to announce [phpMyFAQ 4.1.1](/download), the "Chuck Norris" releases.
This release fixes security vulnerabilities, updates all third party dependencies, and fixes all reported bugs.
[Click here to find a detailed security advisory](/security/advisory-2026-03-31).

[phpMyFAQ 4.1.0](/download), the "Neil Sedaka" release, is here. This release brings a modernized platform with PHP 8.3+ support, TypeScript, Vite 7, Vitest 4, Jodit Editor, Symfony routing in the admin backend, and many new administration features. 
It also adds OpenSearch, .env support, accessibility improvements with dark mode/light mode/high contrast, updated translations, and several experimental features such as FrankenPHP, MCP Server, LDAP group support, CLI updates, and support for PHP 8.5/8.6.

With this release, phpMyFAQ takes a big architectural step forward and is fully prepared for modern PHP environments, enterprise integration, and AI-driven workflows.

We also released [phpMyFAQ 4.0.19](/download), the last bugfix release for the 4.0 branch, which includes all fixes and updates to third party dependencies.

The phpMyFAQ Team is pleased to announce [phpMyFAQ 4.0.18](/download) and [phpMyFAQ 4.1.0-RC.7](/download), the "Jesse Jackson" releases.
These releases fix security vulnerabilities, update all third party dependencies, and fix all reported bugs.
[Click here to find a detailed security advisory](/security/advisory-2026-02-27).

The phpMyFAQ Team would like to announce the availability of [phpMyFAQ 4.1.0-RC.6](/download), the "Eric Dane" releases.
This release fixes all reported bugs, and we updated our third party dependencies.

The phpMyFAQ Team is pleased to announce [phpMyFAQ 4.1.0-RC.5](/download), the "James Van Der Beek" release.
This release fixes all reported bugs, and we updated our third party dependencies.

Today [25 years](/25years) ago, Thorsten Rinne and Bastian Pöttner started with the development of phpMyFAQ. 
We would like to say thank-you to everyone who helped with the development and every one of our users! 
Happy 25th birthday, phpMyFAQ!

The phpMyFAQ Team is pleased to announce [phpMyFAQ 4.1.0-RC.4](/download), the "Rita Süssmuth" release.
This release fixes all reported bugs, and we updated our third party dependencies.

The phpMyFAQ Team would like to announce the availability of [phpMyFAQ 4.0.17](/download) and [phpMyFAQ 4.1.0-RC.3](/download),
the "Renée Good" releases.
These releases fix security vulnerabilities, update all third party dependencies, and fix all reported bugs.
[Click here to find a detailed security advisory](/security/advisory-2026-01-23).

The phpMyFAQ Team is pleased to announce [phpMyFAQ 4.1.0-RC.2](/download), the "Brigitte Bardot" release.
This release fixes all reported bugs, we updated all translations with Claude Code, and we updated our third party 
dependencies.
